Patch Set 1:

Would it make sense to modify the existing function convert_lmd_statx() to just add the correct btime ? Right now I just added another function convert_lmd_statx_btime() to just add the btime only. Because the existing function convert_lmd_statx() appears at other places as well.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).
For more details please see http://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Style_Guidelines

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Definitely we don't want to have another statx() syscall for every file. Adding the btime copy to the existing function is totally fine. 

Please also add a test case for this to confirm it is working properly (ie. "-printf %W@" returns a time that is >= the start of the subtest.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Definitely we don't want to have another statx() syscall for every file. Adding the btime copy to the existing function is totally fine. 
> 
> Please also add a test case for this to confirm it is working properly (ie. "-printf %W@" returns a time that is >= the start of the subtest.

Will add the test.
The existing function convert_lmd_statx() uses lstat_f (lstat) which does not return a btime component. The statx calls only have btime component.

Patch Set 2:

> Uploaded patch set 2.

Just added a test.
Here is my analysis of modifications required in liblustreapi.c to gather correct btime.
btime is gathered by the get_lmd_info_fd() function from MDS.
However, when obtaining the updated stats from the OSTs, 'stat' calls are used. The stat calls don't have a btime field. Therefore they need to be replaced with 'statx' calls. 
Also convert_lmd_statx() need to modified to add a btime assignment to lmd_v2 btime.
Another modification required is the find_time_check() performed after convert_lmd_statx() does not include btime verification (on line 6029). So that needs to be added in find_time_check(), so that it rechecks the btime conditions as well.
Like suggested before, I plan to add a wrapper function to statx (something like, get_updated_stats()) that use statx calls if statx is supported, otherwise defaults to stat calls.
I had a question: I see there is statx_supported function in test-framework.sh which is what I used in my test to check for statx support. Is it a good way to use this to check statx support in my wrapper function or is there a better way?

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

> minor fixups since testing had all failed for an unrelated reason

This test fails for me. 
It does not trim the leading whitespace from attr_printf[*].

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

> This test fails for me.
 > It does not trim the leading whitespace from attr_printf[*].

Hmm, it looks like deleting the initial field leaves an empty string as element [0], and when it is printed there is always the field separator printed, even with ${...[*]## *} that should  strip the leading space. It appears that the leading space is trimmed from each individual field, but doesn't drop the ' ' after the first field.

Patch Set 5:

So as part of integration testing I run this on a rhel7 client / rhel8 server and the otherway around.

And so just in the configuration of rhel8 client this fails with

sanity test_56eab: @@@@@@ FAIL: btime long lfs find ' Wed May 8 01:02:23 2024' != statx 'Wed May 08 01:02:23 2024' 

Looks like the problem is an extra space on rhel8 for the printf format? Can you please look into it? Not sure if whitespace trimming is what you want or if thta's a no-no and it really needs to be exact. The rhel8 is 8.5.

http://testing.linuxhacker.ru/lustre-reports/42531/testresults/sanity1-rhel8client-ldiskfs-DNE-centos7_x86_64-rhel8.5_x86_64/sanity.test_56eab.test_log.oleg342-client.log

The whitespace definitely should not be important to the test.

parents.subject: LU-17705 ptlrpc: replace synchronize_rcu() with rcu_barrier()

commit.subject: LU-16560 utils: add test to crosscheck lfs find and statx btimes
commit.message:
LU-16560 utils: add test to crosscheck lfs find and statx btimes

Added test 56eab that verifies if the lfs find -printf formatted
btimes (human readable and epoch) match with statx btimes.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Anjus George <georgea@ornl.gov>
Change-Id: I9c6201f8adfa100c9462722416f5deec1db70ccc
